expectations.text: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
expectations.evidence: ## No — don't inject `IFormSubmissionAppService` into `ReportAppService` ... calling another module's `IApplicationService` directly across the module boundary is the anti-pattern

expectations.text: Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
expectations.evidence: The right pattern is an ABP **Integration Service**: a separate, narrow contract defined in the producer's `*.Application.Contracts` project, marked `[IntegrationService]`

expectations.text: Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
expectations.evidence: ## 1. Contract — `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` ... The interface lives in the producer's **`*.Application.Contracts`** project (`Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts`).

expectations.text: Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
expectations.evidence: `[IntegrationService]   // hidden from /api, audit-off, no [Authorize] by default`

expectations.text: Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'
expectations.evidence: ## 5. Modular monolith vs microservice — same contract, different transport ... The interface above is deliberately the **same shape** in both deployments. What changes is how DI satisfies it.

expectations.text: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) — NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
expectations.evidence: ## 4. Module wiring — the load-bearing line ... The consumer's `AbpModule` `[DependsOn]` the producer's **`*.Application.Contracts`** module only — never the producer's full `*.Application`. ... `typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule),   // RIGHT — contracts only` ... `// typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationModule),         // WRONG — leaks Forms internals into Reports' DI`
